attributedDescription.content:
Watch this classic collection of Tom and Jerry best moments from  40's, 50's, 60's and 70's episodes!

Episodes Included:
Slicked-Up Pup (1951): Tom accidentally gets Butch's pup dirty and the bulldog threatens to punish the cat if the little dog doesn't stay clean when he gets back.
 
Just Ducky (1953): When a duckling becomes separated from his mama duck, Jerry rescues the duckling from a hungry Tom.
 
Of Feline Bondage (1965): Jerry and Tom end up using scissors to cut the fur off the other after a magical potion of invisibility is used.
 
Down and Outing (1961): Tom and Jerry head out for a fishing trip and do their best to get rid of each other.
 
Dr. Jekyll And Mr. Mouse (1947): Jerry drinks a potion that makes him grow to the size of a giant. He decides to go after Tom, because THIS time he's bigger. Once the potion wears off, Tom drinks some of it, and he gets bigger. They do this over and over again. Academy Award nominee for 1948 Best Short Subject.
 
Castle Wiz (1975): To win $20, Tom and Jerry must stay at a very spooky castle, which people say is haunted. They need the money but doubt they have made the right choice when a pink ghost scares them almost to death--then chases them all through the castle as they desperately try to hide.
 
Trap Happy (1946): Tom hires an exterminator, another cat, to get rid of Jerry.
 
Is There a Doctor in the Mouse? (1964): Drinking a potion he mixed together helps Jerry beat Tom to all the food.
 
Muscle Beach Tom (1956): Tom and Butch try to impress a girl with their muscle-building abilities.
 
Baby Puss (1943): A little girl makes Tom her baby cat, and Jerry along with some alley cats taunts the unfortunate feline.
 
Fine Feathered Friend (1942): Jerry pretends to be a chick so the mother hen protects him from Tom.
 
Kitty Foiled (1948): Tom catches Jerry, but the house canary saves the mouse and then Tom chases both of them.
 
Hatch Up Your Troubles (1949): Jerry is forced to take care of a baby woodpecker and try to avoid Tom while doing it.
 
Landing Stripling (1962): Jerry and a helmet-wearing bird give Tom a difficult time.
 
Mouse For Sale (1955): Jerry gets painted white and sold by Tom, as there is a new desire to get white mice.
 
Little Runaway (1952): Tom plans on capturing an escaped baby seal from the circus for the big reward but Jerry befriends the seal and tries to stop him.
 
The Flying Cat (1952): Jerry and a canary save one another from Tom and take refuge in the canary's birdhouse.
 
Smitten Kitten (1952): Jerry has flashbacks when he sees Tom interested in a female cat.
 
Little Quacker (1950): Jerry saves a baby duck from Tom as the cat prepares to eat it.

Subscribe to Warner Classic:    / @warnerbrosclassics  

Want More Tom and Jerry? https://www.warnerbros.com/brands/tom...

Own the 85th Anniversary Collectors Edition: https://www.amazon.com/Tom-Jerry-85th...

attributedDescriptionBodyText.content:
Watch this classic collection of Tom and Jerry best moments from  40's, 50's, 60's and 70's episodes!

Episodes Included:
Slicked-Up Pup (1951): Tom accidentally gets Butch's pup dirty and the bulldog threatens to punish the cat if the little dog doesn't stay clean when he gets back.
 
Just Ducky (1953): When a duckling becomes separated from his mama duck, Jerry rescues the duckling from a hungry Tom.
 
Of Feline Bondage (1965): Jerry and Tom end up using scissors to cut the fur off the other after a magical potion of invisibility is used.
 
Down and Outing (1961): Tom and Jerry head out for a fishing trip and do their best to get rid of each other.
 
Dr. Jekyll And Mr. Mouse (1947): Jerry drinks a potion that makes him grow to the size of a giant. He decides to go after Tom, because THIS time he's bigger. Once the potion wears off, Tom drinks some of it, and he gets bigger. They do this over and over again. Academy Award nominee for 1948 Best Short Subject.
 
Castle Wiz (1975): To win $20, Tom and Jerry must stay at a very spooky castle, which people say is haunted. They need the money but doubt they have made the right choice when a pink ghost scares them almost to death--then chases them all through the castle as they desperately try to hide.
 
Trap Happy (1946): Tom hires an exterminator, another cat, to get rid of Jerry.
 
Is There a Doctor in the Mouse? (1964): Drinking a potion he mixed together helps Jerry beat Tom to all the food.
 
Muscle Beach Tom (1956): Tom and Butch try to impress a girl with their muscle-building abilities.
 
Baby Puss (1943): A little girl makes Tom her baby cat, and Jerry along with some alley cats taunts the unfortunate feline.
 
Fine Feathered Friend (1942): Jerry pretends to be a chick so the mother hen protects him from Tom.
 
Kitty Foiled (1948): Tom catches Jerry, but the house canary saves the mouse and then Tom chases both of them.
 
Hatch Up Your Troubles (1949): Jerry is forced to take care of a baby woodpecker and try to avoid Tom while doing it.
 
Landing Stripling (1962): Jerry and a helmet-wearing bird give Tom a difficult time.
 
Mouse For Sale (1955): Jerry gets painted white and sold by Tom, as there is a new desire to get white mice.
 
Little Runaway (1952): Tom plans on capturing an escaped baby seal from the circus for the big reward but Jerry befriends the seal and tries to stop him.
 
The Flying Cat (1952): Jerry and a canary save one another from Tom and take refuge in the canary's birdhouse.
 
Smitten Kitten (1952): Jerry has flashbacks when he sees Tom interested in a female cat.
 
Little Quacker (1950): Jerry saves a baby duck from Tom as the cat prepares to eat it.
